title: Error Logging and Monitoring
description: Build a simple error logging system using lists and dicts: create logs, record errors with context, summarize errors by type, and retrieve recent errors.
hints: create_error_log simply returns an empty list []
log_error appends a dict with error_type, message, and context keys
Return the log from log_error so calls can be chained
Use dict.get(key, 0) + 1 pattern to count occurrences in error_summary
Use list slicing [-n:] to get the last n elements
The context parameter defaults to empty string for optional context

testCases.description: Test log_error adds entry to log
testCases.validation:
log = create_error_log()
log_error(log, 'ValueError', 'bad value')
assert len(log) == 1

log = create_error_log()
log_error(log, 'ValueError', 'e1')
log_error(log, 'ValueError', 'e2')
log_error(log, 'TypeError', 'e3')
assert error_summary(log)['ValueError'] == 2

testCases.input:
log = create_error_log()
for i in range(10):
    log_error(log, 'Error', 'msg' + str(i))
len(recent_errors(log, 3))
testCases.description: Test recent_errors returns last n entries
testCases.validation:
log = create_error_log()
for i in range(10):
    log_error(log, 'Error', 'msg' + str(i))
assert len(recent_errors(log, 3)) == 3

log = create_error_log()
log_error(log, 'ValueError', 'bad', 'in parser')
assert log[0]['context'] == 'in parser'

testCases.input:
log = create_error_log()
for i in range(5):
    log_error(log, 'Error', 'msg' + str(i))
recent_errors(log, 2)[0]['message']
testCases.validation:
log = create_error_log()
for i in range(5):
    log_error(log, 'Error', 'msg' + str(i))
assert recent_errors(log, 2)[0]['message'] == 'msg3'
